name: visdiff
purpose: Visual regression verdicts with measured evidence: deterministic browser capture diffed against platform-keyed baselines (SSIM + per-pixel), changed regions with bounding boxes, and a composite diff image.
why: Pixel-exact, repeatable diff of a rendered page - catches sub-visual changes and gives a pass/fail gate.
reach_for_me_when: you changed UI code and are about to claim the page still looks right
you need pixel-measured evidence of what changed on a page, not a screenshot gist
you want a repeatable pass/fail gate on a page's appearance
avoid_when: you just need to eyeball whether two static HTML/CSS files differ - read them directly, you compare markup fine

commands.name: baseline
commands.summary: Capture a page and store it as the baseline for this platform

commands.name: check
commands.summary: Capture and diff against the stored baseline; exit 1 on regression, 4 if no baseline

commands.name: approve
commands.summary: Promote the last check's capture to the new baseline

commands.name: list
commands.summary: List stored baselines and their platform keys

commands.name: install-browser
commands.summary: Explicitly download the chromium binary (the only download path)
